headline: Emerging-Market Rout Deepens as Oil Shock Sparks Investor Flight
summary: MSCI’s equity benchmark for emerging markets tumbled 3%, and is now in correction territory, a level reached when the index is 10% off its most recent high.  The index last week shed 6.9%, sharply underperforming MSCI’s world counterpart, as fears grew that emerging economies will bear the brunt of the Iran war fallout.

content.title: Emerging-Market Rout Deepens as Oil Shock Sparks Investor Flight

The index last week shed 6.9%, sharply underperforming MSCI’s world counterpart, as fears grew that emerging economies will bear the brunt of the Iran war fallout.

headline: Emerging-Market Currencies Pare Drop as Oil, Dollar Surge Fade
summary: A gauge tracking emerging-market currencies trimmed losses to 0.1% after falling as much as 0.8% earlier in the session, with the dollar now trading flat and Brent crude back below $100 per barrel as Group of Seven finance ministers signaled they would take steps to support global energy supplies.  “There is a touch of optimism for some coordination on an oil-reserve release,” said Brendan McKenna, a strategist at Wells Fargo in New York.  The South Korean won and the Brazilian real were among the best-performers, gaining at least 0.5% each.

headline: Emerging Currencies Rebound as Trump Says Iran War May End Soon
summary: A gauge tracking emerging-market currencies gained as much as 0.2% in afternoon trading Monday after Trump said the military operation is “very far” ahead of its initial four- to five-week timeframe.  The late moves pushed a Bloomberg gauge of the greenback down 0.2% while Brent prices erased an earlier surge, trading below $90 per barrel.  Trump’s remarks “signal the intention to end the conflict soon, that it’s getting closer to the objectives of the war, so this is positive for the oil outlook, and hence the global economy,” said Marco Oviedo, senior strategist at XP Investimentos.
